page: Microsoft Azure L64as_v3 is a storage and memory-focused server instance belonging to the LASv3 family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with x86_64 architecture, featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 32 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes 16015 GB of Premium Storage capable capacity. No dedicated GPU hardware is present on this instance.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core and memory performance. The Passmark memory score ranks in the top tier (top 10%), while the stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core score both place in the top 25%. In database-specific testing, the server achieved strong results in Passmark database operations and Redis SET operations, processing 63.44 million operations per second. Large-block memory bandwidth read and write operations perform in the average range, whereas small-block cached read-only memory bandwidth is strong.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the L64as_v3 offers high resource density by combining substantial memory capacity with a massive local storage allocation. This configuration presents a favorable tradeoff for workloads requiring high-capacity local scratch space or fast database indexing without the overhead of external storage arrays. However, the lack of GPU acceleration limits its utility for heavy deep learning training. This instance is suited for large-scale database operations, software compilation, memory-intensive data processing, and multi-threaded decompression tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure L64as_v3 is an x86_64 architecture server in the LASv3 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs across 32 physical cores running at 3.525 GHz, paired with 512.0 GB of RAM and 16015 GB of Premium Storage capable local storage. The instance lacks GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show top-tier memory performance, ranking in the top 10% on Passmark memory tests, alongside strong multi-core CPU capabilities and high-throughput database operations, including 63.44 million Redis SET operations per second. The high density of local storage and memory provides qualitative cost efficiency for data-intensive applications, reducing reliance on external storage. This server is suited for large databases, multi-threaded software compilation, and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L64as_v3 is a storage-optimized server featuring 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 16015 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier memory and strong multi-core database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L64as_v3 features 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 16015 GB of local storage for database and memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity storage and memory-dense platform featuring sixty-four AMD EPYC cores and over sixteen terabytes of local storage.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 vCPUs running at 3.525 GHz
512 GB of system memory with top-tier benchmarked bandwidth
16015 GB of Premium Storage capable local capacity
Strong multi-core performance for software compilation and decompression
Optimized for large-scale database operations and Redis deployments
